entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop used in monocropping and intercropping to study residual effects on subsequent crops.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop used in monocropping and intercropping treatments.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The subsequent crop used to measure the residual effects of previous crops.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by the subsequent maize crop.

entities.name: TOTAL PLANT DRY MATTER
entities.description: The total biomass of the subsequent maize crop.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: The specific type of soil field where the experiment was conducted.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The research institution where the experiment took place.

entities.name: FALLOW
entities.description: A treatment where the land is left unplanted during the previous season.

entities.name: NITROGEN APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of nitrogen fertilizer at levels of 0 and 80 kg N/ha.

entities.name: RESIDUAL EFFECT
entities.description: The influence of a previous crop on the growth and yield of a subsequent crop.

entities.name: MONOCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop species in a field.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously in the same field.

relationships.description: Pigeonpea exhibits a residual effect on subsequent crops
The residual effect is measured on the subsequent maize crop
Pigeonpea was grown using a monocropping method
Pigeonpea was grown using an intercropping method
Sorghum was grown as an intercrop with pigeonpea
Sorghum was grown using a monocropping method
Monocropped pigeonpea increased the grain yield of the subsequent maize crop
Monocropped pigeonpea increased the total plant dry matter of the subsequent maize crop
The Vertisol field is located at ICRISAT
Nitrogen was applied to monocropped and intercropped sorghum
Nitrogen was applied to intercropped pigeonpea
Maize was grown on fallow land as a control treatment
